title: sort_order config block in .kanbus.yml with presets and field overrides
description:
Teams that want to deviate from FIFO defaults can do so via a sort_order block in .kanbus.yml. The primary API is a small set of named presets. Raw field sorting is available as an escape hatch. The schema applies per-status or per-category.

## Rationale

Kanbus is opinionated but not a cage. Some contexts have legitimate reasons to sort differently — a backlog kept in deliberate priority order, for instance, is still a valid discipline if the team commits to maintaining it. The configuration system provides that flexibility while making the FIFO default the zero-config path.

The preset names are chosen to be self-documenting and to communicate the flow intent of each choice. A team choosing priority-first should know they are making a deliberate trade-off against FIFO visibility.

## Presets

- fifo — oldest created_at first (the default; equivalent to no configuration)
- priority-first — sort by priority ascending (critical first), then by created_at ascending as a tiebreaker (FIFO within each priority band)
- recently-updated — sort by updated_at descending; useful for a Blocked column where the team wants to see which blocked items were most recently touched

## Configuration Schema

  sort_order:
    in_progress: priority-first
    categories:
      In progress: recently-updated
    backlog:
      - field: priority
        direction: asc
      - field: created_at
        direction: asc

Status-level configuration takes precedence over category-level configuration. Done-category columns ignore sort_order entirely (they are always last-updated descending).

Scenario: Preset fifo produces oldest-first ordering

Given sort_order for the Discovery status is set to the fifo preset
When the user views the Discovery column
Then issues are ordered oldest created_at first
And this is identical to the default no-configuration behavior

Scenario: Preset priority-first sorts by priority then FIFO within each band

Given sort_order for the in_progress status is set to priority-first
When the user views the In Progress column
Then critical issues appear before high issues, which appear before medium, and so on
And within each priority level issues are ordered oldest created_at first

Scenario: Preset recently-updated shows most recently touched items first

Given sort_order for the blocked status is set to recently-updated
When the user views the Blocked column
Then the issue with the most recent updated_at timestamp is at the top

Scenario: Category-level preset applies to all statuses in that category

Given sort_order categories sets In progress to priority-first
And no status-level sort_order is set for blocked or in_progress
When the user views the In Progress column and the Blocked column
Then both columns sort by priority-first

Scenario: Status-level preset overrides category-level preset

Given sort_order categories sets In progress to priority-first
And sort_order for the blocked status is set to recently-updated
When the user views the Blocked column
Then it sorts by recently-updated (not priority-first)

Scenario: Raw field sort is applied when specified

Given sort_order for backlog specifies a raw sort by priority asc then created_at asc
When the user views the Backlog column
Then issues are sorted by priority ascending and then by created_at ascending as a tiebreaker

Scenario: Done-category column ignores sort_order configuration

Given sort_order for the closed status is set to fifo
When the user views the Done column
Then it still sorts by last-updated descending
And the sort_order configuration for closed is silently ignored

Scenario: An unrecognized preset name produces a validation error

Given sort_order for a status is set to an unrecognized preset name such as custom-magic
When kbs validate is run
Then an error is reported identifying the invalid preset name
And the valid preset names are listed in the error message
creator: ryan.porter
